frameworks.name: value-based decision-making with metacognition
frameworks.one_line: economic choice and confidence dissected into encoding, decoding, and comparison noise

frameworks.name: canonical decision theory (signal-detection / noisy-evidence accumulation)
frameworks.one_line: choice consistency tied to precision of value encoding

frameworks.name: gaze-dependent attentional model of choice
frameworks.one_line: gaze-shift dynamics and endogenous attentional effort shape confidence

core_question: What sources of uncertainty drive confidence reports in value-based choice?
key_assumptions: confidence is a subjective estimate of decision quality
value-based decisions involve separable encoding, decoding, and comparison stages
attentional effort and gaze dynamics modulate choice processing
key_findings: encoding uncertainty does not influence confidence reports
confidence tracks endogenous attentional effort toward alternatives
confidence tracks downstream noise in the comparison process
interpretations: confidence (mis)attributions in value-guided behaviour arise from attentional and comparison-stage noise rather than encoding noise
endogenous attentional states mechanistically guide decisions and metacognitive certainty
